title: Smoky Creamy Dutch Pork Wrap
description: A hearty Dutch-inspired wrap featuring slowroasted pork, a smoky paprika glaze, and a light honeymustard cream sauce.
instructions: Preheat oven to 300°F (150°C). Pat pork cubes dry and season with salt, pepper, smoked paprika, dried thyme, and garlic powder.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over medium‑high heat. Sear pork until browned on all sides, then transfer to a baking dish and add the remaining 1 tbsp oil.
Roast in the oven for 2 ½ to 3 hours, or until the internal temperature reaches 190°F (88°C). Remove pork, let rest 10 minutes, then shred with two forks.
While pork rests, whisk together heavy cream, Dijon mustard, lemon juice, and honey until smooth. Heat gently in a small saucepan until warmed through; keep off the flame.
Warm tortillas in a dry skillet or microwave for a few seconds until pliable.
Spread a thin layer of the honey‑mustard cream over each tortilla, then distribute shredded pork evenly. Fold the ends and roll into a tight wrap.
Serve immediately with a side of fresh parsley or a light cucumber salad.
